easy.description: A container for storing data values that can be changed.
A sequence of characters enclosed in quotes.
A numeric value such as 42 or 3.14.
A data type with two possible values: true or false.
An ordered list of values stored in square brackets.
A block of code designed to perform a task.
A tool used for logging and debugging output.
A keyword used to declare a block-scoped variable.
A keyword used to declare a constant variable.

medium.description: A structure used to repeat code multiple times.
A conditional statement that runs code if a condition is true.
A block that runs when the condition is false.
A statement that outputs a value from a function.
A variable that has been declared but not assigned a value.
A value that represents intentional absence of any object value.
A value representing an invalid number result.
An operator used to determine the type of a value.
A property that returns the size of a string or array.
A method that adds an element to the end of an array.

hard.description: A function that remembers variables from its outer scope.
A function passed into another function and executed later.
An object representing the eventual completion of an async operation.
A keyword used to define an asynchronous function.
Pauses execution until a promise is resolved.
An action or occurrence detected by the program.
A function that waits for and responds to events.
A method that creates a new array by applying a function to each element.
A method that creates a new array with elements that pass a test.
A method that reduces an array to a single value.

expert.description: A mechanism by which JavaScript objects inherit features from one another.
A reference to the current execution context.
A method that sets the value of this in a function.
A method that invokes a function with a given this value.
Similar to call but takes arguments as an array.
JavaScript behavior of moving declarations to the top of scope.
The current context of execution where variables are accessible.
A mechanism that handles asynchronous operations.
A queue with higher priority tasks in the event loop.
A queue with lower priority tasks in the event loop.

god.description: Transforming a function with multiple arguments into a sequence of functions.
A technique to limit how often a function is executed.
A technique to ensure a function is executed at most once in a given time interval.
An optimization technique that caches function results.
A function that can pause and resume execution.
An object that enables iteration over a sequence of values.
A unique and immutable primitive data type.
A collection of key-value pairs where keys are objects held weakly.
An object that intercepts operations on another object.
A built-in object that provides methods for object manipulation.
